incorrects.description: Student placed an incorrect equation on first attempt

steps.text: Let's look closely. Count the rows, then count how many dots are in each row.

incorrects.description: Student placed an incorrect equation on second attempt

params.description: Array highlights row structure. 6 horizontal rows visible.

steps.text: Here's a hint: there are 6 rows. Each row has 3 dots. That gives you the first two numbers in the equation.

incorrects.description: Student placed an incorrect equation three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System counts rows: highlights each of the 6 rows in sequence, showing 3 dots per row.
System places tiles: 6 in groups slot, 3 in items slot, 18 in total slot.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I count the rows. There are 6 rows. I count the dots in one row. There are 3 dots. 6 times 3 equals 18. The equation is 6 times 3 equals 18.
